name: deployment-pipeline-design
description: Design multi-stage CI/CD pipelines with approval gates, security checks, and deployment orchestration. Use when architecting deployment workflows, setting up continuous delivery, or implementing GitOps practices.
capabilities: Defines standard CI/CD stages and ordering for multi-environment pipelines
Provides approval gate patterns for GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Azure Pipelines
Explains deployment strategies including rolling, blue-green, canary, and feature flags
Includes a multi-stage pipeline example with build, test, deploy, and verify steps
Outlines rollback and monitoring practices for safer releases
author: wshobson
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown explanations and YAML/JSON example snippets. All static findings are false positives triggered by example code patterns in documentation. No executable scripts, network calls, filesystem access, or credential handling code exists. The skill is a reference guide for CI/CD architecture patterns.
valueStatement: Many teams deploy without clear stages or approvals and face risky releases. This skill gives proven pipeline structures and rollout strategies to improve safety and speed.
limitations: Provides design guidance and examples, not runnable automation scripts
Referenced assets and reference files are not included in this directory
Examples assume external tools like kubectl, trivy, and Argo Rollouts
Does not configure or manage secrets stores

useCases.title: Multi-stage pipeline plan
useCases.description: Create a staged CI/CD design with approvals and safety checks for production releases.

useCases.title: Standardize deployment strategy
useCases.description: Select rolling, blue-green, or canary strategies for consistent platform releases.

useCases.title: Release governance guide
useCases.description: Define approval gates and verification steps for controlled production changes.

promptTemplates.title: Starter pipeline
promptTemplates.scenario: New team needs basic CI/CD flow
promptTemplates.prompt: Draft a simple multi-stage CI/CD pipeline for a web app with build, test, staging, approval, and production deploy.

promptTemplates.title: Approval gate design
promptTemplates.prompt: Propose approval gate patterns for GitHub Actions and Azure Pipelines, including who approves and when.

promptTemplates.title: Strategy selection
promptTemplates.scenario: Choose a rollout approach for production
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare rolling, blue-green, and canary strategies for a high-traffic API and recommend one.

promptTemplates.title: Full pipeline blueprint
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a full CI/CD pipeline with security scans, staging tests, approval, canary deploy, verification, and rollback.

bestPractices: Fail fast with quick tests before long-running jobs
Use environment parity between staging and production
Automate rollback based on health checks and error rates
antiPatterns: Skipping approval gates for high-risk production changes
Deploying without post-deploy verification or monitoring
Using a single stage for build, test, and deploy

faq.question: What platforms does this work with?
faq.answer: The examples cover GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Azure Pipelines, and the concepts apply to most CI/CD systems.

faq.question: What are the limits of this skill?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and examples only and does not execute or configure pipelines.

faq.question: Can it integrate with existing tools?
faq.answer: Yes, the patterns align with common tools like Kubernetes, Argo Rollouts, and security scanners.

faq.question: Does it access my data or secrets?
faq.answer: No, it is static documentation and does not access files, secrets, or networks.

faq.question: What if my pipeline fails?
faq.answer: Use the rollback and verification guidance to isolate issues and restore a stable release.

faq.question: How is this different from a template skill?
faq.answer: This focuses on architecture decisions and tradeoffs rather than providing runnable templates.
